mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP and rising retail sales compress equity risk premiums by confirming corporate earnings expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in growth-positive periods are not statistically higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate signals aggressive monetary tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points over a 3-month window
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate hike threshold, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate indicate a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns in walk-forward validation, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant across multiple rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals expanding capacity utilization and easing supply bottlenecks, which supports corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with capacity utilization below trend
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates negatively with equity returns during supply shocks, or the forward return differential between high and low INDPRO periods reverses sign in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts reflects weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction investment, which drags down aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5 percent over a 3-month window
failure_condition: Housing starts drops do not correspond to S&P 500 declines, or the signal's information coefficient falls below 0.02 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in consumer price inflation and federal funds rates indicates a stagflationary environment that erodes real corporate margins and forces equity repricing, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The inflation-rate interaction fails to predict negative equity returns, or forward returns remain positive despite simultaneous CPI and rate increases exceeding historical thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms underlying economic momentum and supports earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict short-term equity momentum, or the rank correlation between PAYEMS changes and 5-day forward returns drops below 0.03 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Robust real GDP growth combined with rising housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate exceeds 2.5 percent
failure_condition: High growth fails to support equity returns when valuations are stretched, or the joint signal's predictive power vanishes during periods of elevated market volatility.
